description: Standard inbound inquiry — all major entities present
text:
Hi Sarah,

My name is James Whitfield and I'm the Head of Operations at NovaTech Solutions.
We're a mid-sized logistics company based in Dubai with around 400 employees.
Could we schedule a call this Thursday, June 12th? Our budget is around $45,000 annually.
Feel free to reach me at james.whitfield@novatech.ae or call +971 50 123 4567.

Best, James

description: Multi-person follow-up after demo with deadline and competitors
text:
Hi Marcus,

Following up on our demo. Both Linda Chen (our CFO) and David Park from IT were impressed.
We're comparing you against Salesforce and HubSpot but your pricing is more competitive.
Our goal is to decide by September 30th. The total contract is $120,000.
Please send the proposal to procurement@meridiangroup.com.

Rachel Torres
VP of Sales, Meridian Group

description: Cold outbound reply — clear buying signal and budget
text:
Hi,

Thanks for reaching out. I'm Omar Nassif, Director of Digital Transformation at Gulf Retail Corp.
We want to migrate by January 2025. Our procurement budget is AED 200,000.
Let's talk — omar.nassif@gulfretail.com.

Omar

description: Renewal discussion with contract value and expiry date
text:
Hi,

Our current contract expires on March 31, 2025.
We'd like to renew for two years at $8,500/month.
Please have your account manager contact Lisa Park at lpark@orbitsystems.com.

Tom Nguyen
COO, Orbit Systems

description: RFP response request with phone, budget, and submission deadline
text:
Dear Sales Team,

I'm Dr. Fatima Al-Sayed, Procurement Manager at Saudi National Bank.
We are issuing an RFP for a CRM platform with a budget of SAR 750,000.
Submissions are due by August 15, 2024.
For questions call +966 11 234 5678 or email fatima.alsayed@snb.sa.

Kind regards,
Dr. Fatima Al-Sayed

description: Partnership inquiry between two companies
text:
Hi,

I'm Carlos Mendez, Head of Business Development at Luminary Labs.
We've been following Apex Digital's growth and believe a partnership could work.
Could we schedule an intro call on October 3rd? My direct line is +1 312 555 9090.

Carlos

description: Upsell opportunity — current plan vs proposed plan with discount deadline
text:
Hi Julia,

I wanted to follow up regarding upgrading your Plan A subscription to our Enterprise Suite.
The annual cost would be $24,000, down from $30,000 if you sign before December 1st.

Ben Okafor
Account Manager

description: Intro email with full contact block and location
text:
Hello,

My name is Priya Sharma, VP of Engineering at CloudNest Inc., based in Bangalore.
I came across your product at a recent conference and am interested in a pilot.
You can reach me at priya@cloudnest.io.

Priya

description: Complaint email that still contains CRM-relevant entities

To Whom It May Concern,

I'm Nathan Cole, Operations Director at Horizon Freight.
We've been your client since March 2022 paying $3,200/month.
We've experienced repeated outages and request a credit of $9,600.

nathan.cole@horizonfreight.com
+44 20 7946 0123

Dear Mr. Peterson,

We'd like to invite you and your colleague Mia Hoffman to our summit
on November 14–15, 2024 in Abu Dhabi, hosted by TechForward Group.
Please RSVP to events@techforward.ae by October 25.

Elena Ruiz
Events Director

description: Pricing negotiation with three deal values

Hi,

We've reviewed your proposal of $95,000. We can go up to $78,000 maximum.
If we agree on $82,000 I think we can close this week. Please respond by Wednesday.

Kevin Zhang
CFO, Pinnacle Ventures

description: Enterprise deal signed with legal and procurement contacts
text:
Hi,

Please find attached the signed NDA. We're ready to proceed with the $500,000 enterprise agreement.
Our legal contact is counsel@deltatech.com and procurement is cc'd at po@deltatech.com.
Looking forward to onboarding in Q1 2025.

Anna Lee
CEO, DeltaTech

description: Referral email naming both referrer and new prospect contact

Hi,

I was referred to you by Mark Stevens at BlueRidge Capital.
I'm Sophie Laurent, Head of Finance at Vanta Group in Paris. Budget: €60,000.
My email is s.laurent@vantagroup.fr.

Sophie

description: Support-to-sales handoff with current and target plan pricing

Hi Sales Team,

Support flagged this account for upgrade. Client: Maximus Retail.
Current plan: Starter ($299/month). Eligible for Pro at $799/month.
Contact: michael.osei@maximusretail.com or +233 30 277 1234.

Diane Cho

description: Post-meeting summary with action items and multiple dates
text:
Hi Farouk,

Great meeting today. Summary:
- Crestline Industries will review the proposal by Friday
- Budget confirmed at $180,000 for Year 1
- Technical review with their CTO Hassan Al-Zain on January 8th
- Contract signature targeted for January 31st

Alex

description: Churn risk — client threatening to cancel with competitor mention
text:
Hi,

We're seriously considering cancelling. We're paying $4,500/month and not seeing ROI.
We've been evaluating Zoho CRM as an alternative.
Can someone call me this week? — Robert Finch, CTO, Finch & Co.

description: Invoice dispute with two money values and a date
text:
Dear Accounts Team,

Invoice #INV-2024-0891 for $12,400 dated September 1, 2024 appears incorrect.
We were quoted $10,800. Please advise.

Grace Oduya
Finance Manager, Sterling Logistics
grace@sterlinglog.ng

description: Outbound SDR email with named case studies and price range
text:
Hi Jennifer,

I noticed Brightwave Solutions just expanded to Southeast Asia — congrats!
Companies like yours typically spend $30,000–$50,000 on CRM tools annually.
We've helped TechBloom and DataSphere cut that in half.
Would a 20-minute call on Tuesday work?

Sam Ortega
SDR, NexCRM

description: Legal notice with two companies and a payment deadline

Dear Mr. Hamilton,

This notice is issued by Graystone Legal on behalf of our client Kova Corp.
The outstanding balance of $87,000 must be settled by November 30, 2024.

Natasha Wells
Senior Partner, Graystone Legal
nwells@graystonelegal.com

description: Demo request specifying product modules and team size

Hello,

I'm interested in a demo of your Analytics Pro and Pipeline Manager modules.
I'm Diego Vargas, Sales Director at Comet Technologies in Mexico City.
Team of 25 sales reps, budget around $40,000/year.
Email: d.vargas@comettechnologies.mx

Forwarding this from my colleague Amir Khalil at Orion Systems.
He's based in Cairo and is the right person for their CRM evaluation.
Budget: approximately $55,000. Email: a.khalil@orionsystems.eg.

Yara Hassan

The budget for the CRM implementation has been approved at $220,000

Paula Schneider
Project Manager, Becker Industries

I'd like to schedule a call for next Thursday at 3pm GST (12pm CET).
We're based in Dubai at Nexus Capital.
Please confirm with amira@nexuscapital.ae.

Amira Saleh
Head of Strategy

description: Year-end deal push with discounted price and deadline

As we approach year-end, we can offer a 20% discount if you sign before December 31.
That brings the total to $76,000 from the standard $95,000.

Sarah Kim
AE, TrueScale

Primary contact: Leo Tan, IT Manager at Vertex Group.
leo.tan@vertexgroup.sg | +65 9123 4567

Hi,

We have offices in London, Singapore, and Toronto.
Our London office lead is Claire Fox, Regional Director. Budget: GBP 110,000.
c.fox@globaledge.com | +44 207 123 4567

description: Competitive evaluation with three named tools

We are evaluating your product alongside Zoho, Monday CRM, and Copper.
Decision deadline: June 30. Budget: $60,000/year.
I'm Marcus Webb, VP of Sales at Coda Enterprises.
marcus.webb@codaent.com

description: Onboarding kickoff with four named team members

Hi Team,

Welcome to your onboarding kickoff for FlowDesk Pro.
Your success manager will be Tanya Brown.
Team members: Sarah Park, Dev Lee, and Ravi Mehta.
Kickoff: Monday, March 4th at 10am.

Tanya Brown

description: Security questionnaire with CISO contact and submission deadline

Hi,

As part of our vendor evaluation, we require a completed security questionnaire.
Technical contact: Erik Andersen, CISO at NordCom Group.
e.andersen@nordcom.dk | +45 33 12 3456
Deadline: April 1, 2025.

description: Name only in signature block, absent from email body
text:
Hi,

Just wanted to confirm our meeting is still on for next week.

--
Dr. Amira Al-Hassan
Chief Medical Officer | HealthAxis MENA
Tel: +966 11 456 7890
amira.alhassan@healthaxis.sa
notes: Name only appears in signature — not in body text

description: Three companies — sender / prospect / two competitors
text:
Hi Tom,

This is Alex from Zenith CRM. I noticed Apex Dynamics recently expanded.
We've helped companies move away from Microsoft Dynamics and Pipedrive.

Alex Brennan
Account Executive, Zenith CRM
notes: Own company, prospect company, and two competitors — model must distinguish roles

description: All relative dates — no ISO or calendar dates
text:
Hey,

Hope you're well. We're hoping to wrap this up before the end of the fiscal year.
We close our books in Q4, so ideally a decision within the next 6 weeks.
Early next month works for a call?

Patricia
notes: No concrete dates — all relative expressions. GLiNER likely misses several.

description: Money in three formats: per-unit, computed total, range
text:
Hi,

Just to clarify: 50 users at $35/user/month comes to about $21,000 annually.
Our budget is capped at $15,000–$18,000 for the year. Is there flexibility?

Kevin
notes: Per-unit price, calculated total, and a range — multiple money formats in one message

description: Arabic company name with English translation in parentheses
text:
Hello,

My name is Khalid Al-Mansouri from Advanced Tech Company (شركة التقنية المتقدمة) in Riyadh.
Our technical lead, Eng. Faisal Al-Otaibi, wants a demo.

Khalid
notes: Arabic script alongside English — model should extract English version

description: Decision-maker referred to only by pronoun
text:
Hi,

I spoke with Mike earlier and he said he'd forward this to his boss.
She's the one who makes the final call. Can you follow up with him directly?
notes: Unnamed decision-maker referred to as 'she' — model must not hallucinate a name

description: Entities scattered across a multi-level reply chain
text:
From: nadia.ibrahim@cloudpeak.io
Subject: Re: Re: Proposal follow-up

Yes, let's do Friday at 2pm.

> Following up on the $80,000 proposal sent to CloudPeak on March 3rd.
> Confirm with Nadia or Ben Strauss.

>> Ben is on leave until April 10th. I'll be contact for now.
notes: Entities spread across quoted reply levels — indentation adds noise

description: Role implied by context, contact unnamed
text:
Hi,

I spoke to the person who handles software procurement at Vertex Corp.
She didn't give her name but said budget is $25,000 and they want to decide before Q2.

Ahmed
notes: Procurement contact unnamed — role is described contextually, not titled

description: Job title embedded in sentence before name, no punctuation separator
text:
Hi,

Our chief revenue officer Helena Voss will be joining the call.
She oversees all deals above $100,000 at Crescent Software.

Best
notes: Title immediately precedes name without comma or colon

description: Two people with the same last name at different companies
text:
Hi,

I've cc'd David Chen from our team and Jennifer Chen from Bridgeway Corp.
They'll both need to approve the $50,000 contract by Friday.
notes: Same last name — model must distinguish two separate people

description: Two phone formats — dashes vs spaces, two country codes
text:
Hi,

You can reach us at +1-800-555-0199 (US) or +971 4 123 4567 (Dubai).

Support Team, GlobalServ
notes: International numbers in different formatting styles

description: Deal value with percentage discount — two resulting amounts
text:
Hi,

We're offering a 15% discount on the list price of $100,000,
bringing your cost to $85,000. Valid until March 15.

Ben
notes: Percent is not a money entity; list price and discounted price are

description: Company referred to as 'the company' after first mention
text:
Hi,

Following our call with Hartmann AG last week, I confirm the company is moving forward.
Contact is Klaus Bauer, budget is €90,000. He should get back to us by Thursday.
notes: Model needs to extract from first mention — not the pronoun

description: Two functional email addresses not paired with named individuals
text:
Hi,

Please send the contract to contracts@legaldept.io and copy billing@financeunit.org.
I'm Wendy Cross, Head of Procurement at Surge Dynamics.

Wendy
notes: Emails belong to teams/departments, not named contacts

description: Location embedded in company name AND as standalone city
text:
Hi,

I represent Cairo Capital Group, headquartered in Cairo.
We also have offices in London and New York.

Hana Abdelaziz
notes: 'Cairo' appears in company name and as a separate location entity

description: Competitor mentioned positively — still a competitor signal
text:
Hi,

We've used Salesforce for 5 years and love it, but we need better integrations.
Looking at your platform as a complement, not replacement. Budget: $15,000.
— James Ford, COO, FordWorks
notes: Client likes Salesforce but is looking for a complement — still extract it

description: Two separate deals in one email with different amounts and locations
text:
Hi,

The $30,000 deal for the Dubai office closes Friday.
The $120,000 contract for the London office goes through legal first — ready by March 20.

Claudia Renz
VP Finance
notes: Two distinct deal values tied to two different locations

description: Hyphenated surname with academic title
text:
Dear Prof. Marie-Claire Beaumont,

We're pleased to offer a research partnership to the University of Lyon.
The grant is €45,000 and runs January to December 2025.

Henri Dupont
Partnership Manager
notes: Academic title + hyphenated surname — common in European contacts

description: Same date in EU and US format in the same sentence
text:
Hi,

Could we meet on 15/04/2025 (or April 15, 2025 for US colleagues)?
Preferred time 14:00 GST. — Tariq Mahmoud, Mosaic Group
notes: 15/04/2025 and April 15, 2025 refer to the same day — two formats

description: Cc line contains relevant contacts not named in body

To: sales@ourcrm.com
Cc: ana.ferreira@cortex.pt, rlopes@cortex.pt
Subject: Demo request

Hi, I'd like to schedule a demo for our team at Cortex. Budget TBD.

Miguel Santos
notes: Two additional contacts only appear in the Cc header, not the email body

description: Money with non-standard currency text abbreviation
text:
Hi,

Our budget is approximately 2.5 million EGP, or about 50,000 USD at current rates.
Please quote in both currencies.

Noha
notes: Currency expressed in text (EGP) and abbreviation (USD)

description: Company name is an acronym only
text:
Hi,

I'm reaching out from MENA FSI — our regional fintech arm, based in Bahrain.
Budget: $200,000–$300,000.

Nour Qassem
Head of Technology

description: Date as fiscal quarter with year
text:
Hi,

We are targeting go-live by Q3 FY2025. Hard deadline is Q4 FY2025.
Please confirm feasibility by end of this month.

Regards
notes: FY-qualified quarter — not a calendar date, GLiNER may miss these

description: Person name that looks like a product/brand name

Hi,

Our contact at Fusion Labs is Max — he runs the Fusion Analytics suite.
He's also evaluating your product against Fusion CRM (a different company).

Dana
notes: 'Fusion' appears as a person's nickname context, company name, product, and competitor

description: Contract value as monthly rate × duration
text:
Hi,

We're looking at a 24-month contract at $5,500/month — that's $132,000 total.
Can you match that?

Thanks, Ian
notes: Rate and total both present — both are relevant money entities

description: Informal company alias in quotes vs full legal name

Hi,

We're from 'The Lab' (officially Emergent Laboratory Solutions Inc.).
Our CEO, Prof. Zhang Wei, would like to speak with your enterprise team.

lab@emergent.io | +86 21 1234 5678

description: Forwarded email — sender in header, different contact in body

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Sarah Osei <sarah@brightgroup.gh>

Hi, we're Bright Group in Accra. Budget: GHS 400,000.
Contact our IT Director, Patrick Mensah, for technical questions.
patrick@brightgroup.gh | +233 24 456 7890

description: Internal project codename used — not a product name
text:
Hi,

We're calling this 'Project Hydra' internally — our move to a new CRM.
Budget: $250,000. Sponsor is our CPO, Vanessa Obi at StormEdge Technologies.

Vanessa
notes: 'Project Hydra' is an internal initiative name, not a product to extract

description: Proposal with start and end date in one range expression
text:
Hi,

The proposal is valid from October 1 to October 31, 2024. Please sign before expiry.

Marco Bellini
Sales Director, Ardent Systems
notes: Two dates in one range — model should catch both endpoints

description: Person mentioned only as a past contact, not current stakeholder

Hi,

We previously worked with your colleague Tom Baker about two years ago.
Is he still with the company? We'd like to reconnect.

Elsa Vogel
Head of Partnerships, Velo Corp
notes: Tom Baker is a historical reference — still an extractable person entity

description: One-liner — no entities expected
text: Can we push to next week?
notes: Minimal signal. Model should extract nothing.

description: Heavy abbreviations — acronym-dense corporate speak
text: FYI — touched base w/ PMO at ADNOC re: ERP RFP. Want demo ASAP, EOD Tue. POC is SME from IT. ~SAR 500K budget.
notes: PMO, RFP, POC, SME, EOD — GLiNER likely misses most

description: Company names that are common English words
text: We've been using Apple, Notion, and Monday for our ops but want something more integrated. Our team at Target has about 200 people.
notes: 'Apple', 'Notion', 'Monday', 'Target' — all real companies AND common words

description: Name/place ambiguity — Jordan and Paris are both persons and locations
text:
Hi,

Jordan from Phoenix Consulting here. We're working with Paris (our project lead) and the team in Jordan (the country) to finalize the deal.
notes: 'Jordan' is person + country. 'Paris' is person + city. Severe ambiguity.

description: Auto-generated system notification — no human entities
text: This is an automated notification from your CRM system. Lead #4821 was updated on 2024-10-15 at 09:32 UTC. Stage changed from Qualified to Proposal Sent.
notes: System message — nothing extractable

description: Numbers everywhere but none are CRM-relevant
text:
Hi,

We have 3 locations, 12 departments, 450 employees, and 7 product lines. System ID: 8842-A. Founded in 1998. Response time: 48 hours.
notes: No money, no named entities, no meaningful dates — over-extraction risk

description: Email entirely in Arabic with no English entities
text:
مرحباً،

أنا أحمد الشمري من شركة التقنية الحديثة في الرياض.
نودّ معرفة المزيد عن منتجاتكم.

مع التحية
notes: Entirely Arabic text — English-trained GLiNER expected to extract nothing

description: Out-of-office autoreply — minimal extractable entities
text: Thank you for your email. I am out of office from October 21 to October 28 with limited access. For urgent matters contact support@company.com.
notes: OOO reply — dates and one email present, no person name or company

description: Dense financial metrics — no deal-level entities
text: Q3 revenue was $4.2B, up 12% YoY. EBITDA margin: 23.4%. Operating costs: $1.1B. Cash position: $800M.
notes: Financial report numbers are not deal values

description: Obvious spam with fake but extractable entities
text:
Dear Valued Customer,

Congratulations! You've been selected to receive $5,000,000 from the United Nations Development Fund. Contact Mr. John Williams at reward@un-special.info to claim.
notes: Spam — entities are present but fictitious. Model should still extract what's there.

description: Pronoun-only, no antecedent in this message
text: Can you update their address and resend them the invoice?
notes: 'Their' and 'them' — no referent in this message

description: Meeting notes with roles but no names
text:
Meeting notes — Oct 10:
- The CTO expressed concern about integration
- The CFO asked about pricing flexibility
- The VP of Sales wants a pilot by end of year
- Legal will review the contract next week
notes: Titles present but no names — model should not invent names

From: noreply@crm.internal
Sent: Monday, October 14, 2024 9:15 AM
To: team-sales@internal.com

Lead assigned to queue. No action required.

Best

notes: Two separate company deal values in one short message

description: Person name in possessive form

notes: 'Group 7' is the company name — not a numbered list item

description: Money expressed in words, no numeric or symbol

description: Person referred to only by title, no name given

description: Three email addresses with no names attached
text: Send the contract to ceo@alphatech.com and copy legal@alphatech.com and accounts@thirdparty.org.
notes: Functional/team emails — no person name pairing

description: Competitor switch with no explicit prospect company name
text: They were with Dynamics 365 for 3 years but came to us. Budget: $90,000/year.
notes: Only competitor and deal value — no company name for the prospect

description: Plan tier names that are also common nouns
text: Upgrade them from Basic to Pro+. They want the Analytics add-on too. New total: $2,400/month.
notes: 'Basic', 'Pro+', 'Analytics' — product tier names vs generic words

description: City in company name vs standalone location
text: Reach out to London Capital Group at their Canary Wharf office and their branch in Manchester.
notes: 'London' is part of company name AND a standalone location

description: Deal note mixed with personal context
text: Quick note — met Ahmad at his daughter's school event. He's CISO at NovaBridge. Budget unclear, probably 6 figures.
notes: Vague money expression — 'six figures' with no precise number

description: Acronym used alongside full company name in parentheses
text: Follow up with the guys at MTSC (MidTech Solutions Corp) — spoke to Raj last week about the $45k deal.
notes: Model should extract full name over acronym

description: Two phone number formats including letter-encoded toll-free
text: Call Yousef on +971-55-100-2233 or the office line 800-NEXUS (800-63987).
notes: Standard international format and US toll-free with letter encoding

description: Date as 'next [month name]' without a specific day
text: Let's target close in next October. The Navara Group deal should be around $175,000.
notes: Month name without a day — vague future date

description: Two people with the same first name at different companies
text: Both Davids confirmed — David Kim at Polaris and David Osei at TrueForm. Combined deals total $80,000.
notes: Two different Davids — model must keep them distinct

description: Specific product names of two competitors
text: They compared us against Salesforce Sales Cloud and Microsoft Dynamics 365 Sales. Our price beat both.
notes: Full product names not just company names

description: Budget cut — original and reduced amounts
text: They got their budget cut from $200k to $130k. Still moving forward. Contact is Ting Wu, Procurement Manager.
notes: Two money values representing before and after a budget reduction

description: Two currencies for two office locations in one deal
text: Contract is GBP 40,000 for the UK team and AED 150,000 for the Dubai office. Contact is Nina Clarke.
notes: Two deal values in different currencies tied to different locations

description: Very long compound job title
text: The decision-maker is their Senior Vice President of Global Sales Operations, James Okoro. Reach him at james@globalcorp.com.

job title: Senior Vice President of Global Sales Operations

notes: Model may truncate or split the long title

description: Pure action command — no entities
text: Show me all open deals
notes: Intent-only. Nothing to extract.

description: Pronoun with no antecedent
text: Can you update their contact info?
notes: 'Their' has no referent in this message

description: Company names that are common English phrases
text: We're talking to The Agency and Open Door about a partnership. Budget TBD.
notes: Ambiguous names that could be generic phrases

description: Numbers present but none are CRM-relevant
text: The client has 5000 employees, 3 offices, and their system ID is #88420.
notes: Numbers everywhere — none are money, dates, or named entities

description: General best-practice question with no entities
text: What's the best way to handle a stalled deal?
notes: No specific entities — general question

description: Instruction referencing a deal by ID only
text: Update deal #4821 — change the close date to next Friday.
notes: Deal referenced by number — no company or person name

description: Entirely lowercase with no capitalization cues
text: update the deal for acmecorp the cfo reached out and said budget is around 50 grand closing next month
notes: No capitals to help NER — tests robustness to casing

description: Emoji-heavy message with entities hidden between emojis
text: 🔥 New lead alert! 🎯 Ahmed from TechBase 💰 budget $30k 📅 call Friday
notes: Emojis as structural tokens — entities embedded between non-text characters

description: Sarcastic message — entities present but context is ironic
text: Oh great, another $1,000,000 deal with Google falling into our lap. Sure.
notes: Sarcasm — model should still extract entities regardless of ironic framing

description: Hypothetical scenario — not a real deal
text: Imagine we closed a $500,000 deal with Amazon tomorrow. What would our commission be?
notes: Hypothetical framing — model should extract entities regardless

description: Negative statement — deal explicitly did not happen
text: We did NOT get the NovaTech deal. They chose Oracle. Budget was $250,000.
notes: Negation — deal is lost but entities are still CRM-relevant

description: Heavy slang — informal sales shorthand
text: yo the bd guy @ apex hit us up lmk if u want me to ping him back
notes: 'bd guy' = business development, '@' = at company

description: Internal team discussion with no prospect entities
text: Can someone take the 3pm call today? I have a conflict. Assign to whoever is free.
notes: No lead, deal, or contact entities — purely internal

description: Filter query with threshold values — no specific deal entities
text: Filter deals where amount is greater than $100,000 and close date is before Q2.
notes: Dollar amount and quarter as filter values, not deal-specific entities

description: Deal referred to only by internal nickname
text: Any update on the 'whale' deal? Last I heard they were close to signing.
notes: 'The whale' is an internal nickname — no company, person, or value

description: Message with only time references, no named entities
text: We need to close at least 3 deals before end of Q4. Monthly targets are due by the 5th. Annual review is in December.
notes: Dates present but no companies, people, or money

description: Ambiguous 'them' — no referent in this message
text: Call them back and let them know we accept their counteroffer.
notes: 'Them' and 'their' have no antecedent here

description: Generic lowercase product/feature names
text: They want the basic package with reporting and the automation add-on.
notes: Product names that are lowercase common nouns — boundary unclear

description: Website URL that is not an email address
text: Check out their website at www.acmegroup.com — they updated their pricing page.
notes: Model should not extract a URL as an email address

description: All-caps message — no lowercase signals for NER
text: CALL BACK DANIEL AT NEXCORP NOW BUDGET IS 80K THEY ARE LEAVING
notes: All caps throughout — tests robustness to aggressive casing

description: Channel partner intro email with reseller and end-customer named
text:
Hi,

I'm writing on behalf of our reseller partner BridgeTech.
Their client, Falcon Industries, is interested in a 3-year deal worth $340,000.
Primary contact at Falcon is Yusuf Al-Farsi, VP of IT, yusuf@falconindustries.ae.
BridgeTech account manager is Lena Schulz, +49 30 1234 5678.

Best

description: Person's name spelled inconsistently across two paragraphs
text:
Hi,

Mohammed Al-Otaibi from Crescent Group called yesterday about the renewal.

Just a reminder that Mohamed has a hard deadline of March 31 and budget is SAR 180,000.

Please follow up ASAP.
notes: 'Mohammed' in first paragraph, 'Mohamed' in second — two spellings, one person

description: Deal note with a cc'd legal contact and two dates
text: Log this: Titan Corp deal at $415,000 is in legal review. Contract to be sent to legal@titancorp.com by April 5, expected signature by April 20. Contact: Priya Nair, General Counsel.

description: Company name is a single common noun with no other context
text: Just spoke to the team at Horizon. Their budget is $55,000 and they want to go live in Q3.
notes: 'Horizon' alone as company — extremely common word, high ambiguity

description: Message containing a person's name that is also a product name
text: Can you upgrade their plan from Max to Ultra? The account owner is Max.
notes: 'Max' is a person but also a common product/tier name — tests disambiguation
